N
Nu-Map Docs
Developer Reference
← Back to Nu-Map
nu-map/documentation / src/ / data/ / persistence.js

persistence.js Data module

Four thin functions that wrap localStorage for the main plan state. The storage key is derived from institution.storagePrefix so institution forks never collide in the same browser. All logic for what to save lives in PlannerContext — this module only handles serialization and key construction.

Parent
data/
Called by
PlannerContext.jsx (on mount and every state change), Header.jsx, LoadingScreen.jsx (storageKey only)
Key format
{storagePrefix}-state-v2 — namespace from IInstitution, version suffix for schema migration

Exported functions

storageKey(prefix: string) → string
Builds the namespaced localStorage key for the main plan state. Returns `${prefix}-state-v2`. Exported separately so Header and LoadingScreen can clear the key without importing the full module. The schema version suffix (-state-v2) must be bumped when the saved state shape changes in a breaking way.
Returns: string — e.g. "ncp-state-v2" for Northeastern
loadSaved(prefix: string) → SavedState | null
Reads the saved planner state from localStorage. Returns the parsed object if found, or null on first visit or after a reset. The caller (PlannerContext) is responsible for providing defaults when null is returned.
Returns: Parsed SavedState object, or null if nothing saved
saveState(prefix: string, persist: boolean, obj: object) → void
Persists the planner state. When persist = true, writes { persist: true, ...obj }. When persist = false, writes only { persist: false } — this sentinel tells the next loadSaved() call not to restore the state. localStorage quota errors are swallowed silently.
clearState(prefix: string) → void
Removes the plan state key from localStorage. Called when the user resets the planner. The theme and other permanent preferences live under separate keys managed directly in PlannerContext and are not cleared by this function.

localStorage key structure

This module manages only the main state blob under a single key. All other keys (theme, zoom, cohort settings, plan index, etc.) are managed directly in PlannerContext via the key(name) helper which produces ${storagePrefix}-${name}.

KeyContentsManaged by
{prefix}-state-v2Full serialized plan state (placements, cohort, major, settings)persistence.js
{prefix}-themeTheme name stringThemeContext
{prefix}-zoomNumber (zoom level)PlannerContext directly
{prefix}-ent-sem / -ent-yearEntry semester/yearPlannerContext directly
{prefix}-grad-sem / -grad-yearGrad semester/yearPlannerContext directly
{prefix}-starredstring[] of starred courseIdsPlannerContext directly
{prefix}-seen-disclaimer"1" when dismissedDisclaimerModal directly
{prefix}-plan-indexPlan list metadataPlannerContext directly
{prefix}-active-planActive plan IDPlannerContext directly
{prefix}-plan-data-{id}Per-plan placements/majors JSONPlannerContext directly
ℹ
PlannerContext accesses most keys directly using a key(name) helper: const key = n => `${storagePrefix}-${n}`. Only the main state blob (plan-data, index, active) goes through the module-level pattern above. This separation keeps the module minimal while giving PlannerContext full control over key lifecycle.

State shape (what saveState writes)

{
  "persist":      true,
  "entSem":       "fall",  "entYear":  2026,
  "gradSem":      "spring", "gradYear": 2031,
  "placements":   { "CS3500": "fall2027", ... },
  "specialTermPl": { "coop-1234": { "typeId": "coop", "semId": "sumA2028", ... } },
  "semOrders":    { "fall2027": ["CS3500", "CS1800"] },
  "shOverrides":  { "CS1234": 3 },
  "bonusSH":      { "fall2026": 8 },
  "major":        "2024/computer-information-science/computer_science_bscs",
  "conc":         null,
  "minor1":       null,
  "placedOut":    ["CS1200"],
  "substitutions": [{ "from": "ENGW3302", "to": "ENGW3315" }]
}
